diagrams.title: CUDA Vector Addition Execution Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the complete workflow from host memory allocation to kernel execution and result retrieval
elements: Host allocates arrays a, b, c
cudaMalloc allocates device memory d_a, d_b, d_c
cudaMemcpy H2D copies a, b to device
Kernel launch creates grid of thread blocks
Each thread computes c[i] = a[i] + b[i]
cudaMemcpy D2H copies results back to host
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the complete host-device interaction pipeline for vector addition, understanding data movement and parallel execution phases.

diagrams.title: Thread-Block-Grid Mapping for Vector Addition
diagrams.description: Illustrates how threads, blocks, and grid map to array indices in CUDA
elements: Thread k, Block b → index = b × blockDim + k
diagrams.purpose: Understand the hierarchical thread organization and global index calculation formula for mapping parallel threads to sequential array elements.

diagrams.title: Memory Access Patterns: Coalesced vs Non-Coalesced
diagrams.description: Comparison of efficient coalesced access vs inefficient strided access in vector addition
elements: Coalesced: Thread 0 reads a[0], Thread 1 reads a[1]... (contiguous)
Single memory transaction for warp (32 threads)
Non-coalesced: Thread 0 reads a[0], Thread 32 reads a[32]... (strided)
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate why memory access pattern critically affects GPU performance and how to write coalesced memory access patterns.
